__fixture_tag__: VENDORED UNIVERSAL-FF PREDICTION — frozen NNP output, not measurement
target_property: frequency-conversion efficiency at modulation rate in a time-domain photonic metamaterial — ITO sub-ps optical pump or graphene gate-modulated permittivity ε(ω, t) — proxy for the time-varying-medium nonlinear-optics response (Engheta 2020 + Caloz 2020 reviews)
predicted_modulation_unit: GHz (effective electrical-bias modulation rate of time-varying ε)

operating_conditions.host: ITO thin-film (epsilon-near-zero regime, λ ~ 1240 nm) under sub-ps pulsed optical pump OR graphene-on-silicon-photonic-waveguide gate-modulated
operating_conditions.drive: femtosecond optical pump (1030 nm Yb-fiber laser, ~ 100 fs pulse duration, fluence 1-10 mJ/cm²) for ITO; RF gate modulation (CMOS-compatible) for graphene

model_caveat: Universal FFs predict atomistic energies / forces on the photonic-substrate lattice — they do NOT directly compute time-varying dielectric ε(ω, t) or photonic-mode frequency-conversion (which is a Maxwell-equation + density-matrix dynamics calculation in the time-varying-medium framework). The 8% / 80 GHz values here are a PROXY-from-literature pinned with raw#10 C3 honesty: best-reported ITO + graphene time-modulated permittivity demonstrations at lab scale 2020-2025. SEMI-QUANTITATIVE — NNP is used for dielectric ε(ω) extraction on the static lattice geometry only.
proxy_source: Engheta 2020 Nature Photonics 14:686 (time-varying photonic-metamaterial review — frequency conversion via abrupt-temporal-boundary scattering); Caloz et al. 2020 Engineering 6:1019 (space-time engineered metasurfaces review — modulation-rate vs efficiency trade-off); Alam et al. 2016 Science 352:795 (ITO epsilon-near-zero nonlinear response, sub-ps temporal modulation, modest efficiency); Pendry et al. 2021 Optics Express 29:39811 (time-varying-medium photon up/down-conversion theory); Sounas + Alù 2017 Nature Photonics 11:774 (non-reciprocity from time-modulation, foundational); Engheta UPenn + Alù CUNY Photonics group corpus 2020-2025
falsifier_text: frequency-conversion efficiency < 10 % OR modulation rate < 100 GHz → FAIL
unproven_markers_preserved: time-modulated permittivity drive-power HARD_WALL — the 10% / 100 GHz target requires drive intensities that exceed the dielectric-breakdown threshold of ITO + graphene at sustained-modulation operation; pulsed-mode demonstrations only (Alam 2016 + Engheta 2020) — CW or quasi-CW operation UNVERIFIED

ITO epsilon-near-zero response is wavelength-window-narrow (~ 1200-1300 nm) — telecom C-band 1530-1565 nm operation requires alternative ENZ material (AlZnO / cadmium oxide) UNVERIFIED at the 8% efficiency floor
graphene gate-modulation rate > 100 GHz UNVERIFIED at the photon-conversion-efficiency target (gate-RC bandwidth limits typical to 10-50 GHz at the >1 cm² area scale)
time-domain photonic-metamaterial frequency-conversion beyond ~ 10 GHz at > 5% efficiency UNVERIFIED at commodity SOI-photonic process (Engheta + Caloz 2020 review — lab-scale demonstrations only)
device-layer integration + lithography ⇒ hexa-chip per CROSS_LINK §3.2; this candidate is the MATERIAL-LAYER claim only
no claim of RT-SC or ambient-pressure novel-phase transitions — pure photonic time-modulation
honesty_caveats: MARGINAL on both axes — 8% efficiency below the 10% F-TDMETA-1 target, and 80 GHz below the 100 GHz target. Both arms of F-TDMETA-1 are within the empirical 2020-2025 best-reported lab-scale demonstrations but BELOW the falsifier-PASS threshold. Falsifier_relation = MARGINAL because both axes are close to but do not clear the FAIL boundary; SPEC_FIRST preserved — DOES NOT promote to EXTERNAL-VERIFIED. The time-modulated permittivity drive-power HARD_WALL is the binding constraint per Engheta 2020 + Caloz 2020 reviews — drive intensities to reach 10% / 100 GHz exceed dielectric-breakdown thresholds at sustained-modulation operation.
license_note: Peer-reviewed paper values per Nature Photonics + Science + Optics Express publisher terms; Engheta UPenn + Alù CUNY Photonics + Caloz EPFL lab authority; STMicroelectronics + IMEC SOI-photonic vendor context (raw#10 C3, NOT n=6 lattice-fit).
